behavior_summary: This script implements a U8P_DECEIVER::look/examine handler for an in-game entity named 'Deceiver'.
When the player uses the 'U8P_DECEIVER::look' or examine action on this entity, the process emits a single short bark text: "Deceiver ".
After emitting the bark the process suspends/ends; there is no branching, state mutation, menu, or further logic in the handler.
The script appears purely descriptive: it labels or identifies the NPC/object when inspected and does not interact with global flags or external systems.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: entry -> emit Bark("Deceiver ") -> suspend/end.
No conditional branches, loops, menus, or external calls observed in the U8P_DECEIVER::look handler.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "Deceiver " intentional (formatting) or an artifact of extraction?
Does any other script or game system reference this entity's item/weapon properties (metadata present but unused by U8P_DECEIVER::look)?
Is the suspend a normal end-of-process for U8P_DECEIVER::look handlers in this engine, or does it imply the process yields for further engine-driven behavior?

main_function.name: U8P_DECEIVER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/text when the player examines this NPC/object by emitting a single bark identifying it as 'Deceiver'.
entry_conditions: Player invokes the 'U8P_DECEIVER::look' or examine action on the entity.
ProcessType 0x0208 indicates this is a U8P_DECEIVER::look-type process handled by the engine.
Function concludes with a suspend (inferred end-of-U8P_DECEIVER::look behavior).

function_map.name: U8P_DECEIVER::look
function_map.role: Handle the U8P_DECEIVER::look/examine event; emit a short identifying bark and then suspend.
